priority_changes: Make .xlsx parseable (C1) and add Formulas sheet (C2) - high priority
Add cell->embedded_packet mapping (C3) and key_calculations.csv (C4) - high priority
Memo updates and monitoring plan (C5, C6) - medium priority
verification_steps_after_revision: 1) Confirm openpyxl can load tunnel_smoke_control.xlsx and that key formula cells match key_calculations.csv values.
2) Confirm cell->embedded_packet mapping entries exist and correspond to the memo row-level citations.
3) Re-run deterministic precheck and include precheck_report.txt with submission.

planned_changes.description: Ensure the .xlsx workbook is machine-parseable by openpyxl: re-export or rebuild the workbook so formulas are native spreadsheet formulas (no images) and metadata are standard. Run a local openpyxl load test before submission.
Add a 'Formulas' worksheet (or a separate 'formulas.txt' artifact) that lists the key calculation cells and the exact formulas used (one formula per line with sheet!cell reference).

Add an explicit cell-to-packet mapping sheet or column: for every derived cell used in the memo decision table, include a mapping entry sheet!cell -> embedded_packet.exact_path (e.g., 'Sheet1!C12 -> embedded_packet.Z2.measured_velocity_m_s').
Export key calculation cells (inputs, formula, output) to a CSV (key_calculations.csv) and attach it as an artifact so verifiers can reproduce numeric checks without loading the .xlsx.

Update the acceptance memo: replace shorthand field names with exact embedded_packet paths in the Embedded Packet Inputs table and row-level citation column; add the file names/paths of the new verification artifacts (Formulas sheet, key_calculations.csv) and note that a local openpyxl parse test was performed.
Expand the memo recommendation to include a concise monitoring plan (metrics, thresholds, responsible party, frequency) and add explicit sign-off lines (name/title/date).

Add a short 'Verification Notes' section in the memo describing any modeling assumptions (e.g., Z3 no direct effect) and listing exactly which rubric items were re-tested after the artifact updates (especially ri_2).

Before re-submission, run deterministic artifact precheck locally (or via CI) to confirm both the .xlsx and CSV/formulas exports are readable by the verifier environment; include a brief precheck report artifact (precheck_report.txt).

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve verifiability and traceability: ensure workbook parseability for verifiers, add exact embedded_packet path citations, and supply plaintext exports of formulas/calculations so rubric item ri_2 (reproducible calculations and sensitivity) can be fully verified.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Improve verifiability and traceability: ensure workbook parseability for verifiers, add exact embedded_packet path citations, and supply plaintext exports of formulas/calculations so rubric item ri_2 (reproducible calculations and sensitivity) can be fully verified.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and memo clearly states acceptance criteria, simulated states, decision table, and recommendation (accept reverse_fan_mode + close_ramp). Key weaknesses: the workbook (.xlsx) could not be parsed in the verifier environment (openpyxl_unavailable), preventing verification of formulas, intermediate calculations, and cell-level citations; the memo uses shorthand field names rather than exact embedded_packet paths which reduces machine-traceability; verifier flagged an internal metadata inconsistency in the grader output regarding ri_2 (appears both passed and failed). These issues reduce verifier confidence in semantic judgments, especially for the reproducible calculations / sensitivity rubric item (ri_2).
